items.title: New NT police public safety officers 'fully trained' in carrying guns

children.content: The Northern Territory Police Force has moved to reassure Territorians that new, armed public safety officers due to hit the beat from today are fully trained to carry guns.

items.title: Thousands watch on as boats made of beer cans race on Darwin beach

children.content: Competitors at the annual Beer Can Regatta race rafts engineered from drinking containers, with beer or soft-drink cans the popular choice this year.

image.altText: Group of people watch a beer can boat race at the beach

items.title: Young NT cattle ringers conquer outback obstacle course

children.content: Based on the day in the life of an outback station ringer, the Battle of the Barkly obstacle course has been won by two young cattle industry workers.

image.altText: Man celebrates winning a competition while doing a lap of honour on a desert horse racing tack

items.title: The artefacts that tell the story of one of Australia's most notorious cases

children.content: Forty years ago, the chance discovery of a bloodied matinee jacket unravelled a murder conviction. Now, that evidence lives inside a national museum's collection that's too sensitive for public display.

gallery.altText: A museum curator wearing blue surgical gloves, holding a box which has a baby girl's pink outfit laid flat inside.
A small, dirty knit jacket laid flat on tissue paper.
A black and white photo of a couple outside court, they are holding up a large photo of Lindy Chamberlain holding baby Azaria.

thumbnail.altText: A museum curator wearing blue surgical gloves, holding a box which has a baby girl's pink outfit laid flat inside.
A small, dirty knit jacket laid flat on tissue paper.
A black and white photo of a couple outside court, they are holding up a large photo of Lindy Chamberlain holding baby Azaria.

items.title: Darwin vacation care worker charged with indecently touching child

children.content: NT Police allege the male educator, 40, indecently touched a child under the age of 16 at a vacation care program in Darwin.

image.altText: An NT Police Car parked on the street.

items.title: A speeding shadow minister puts the brakes on NT Labor’s rebuild

children.content: Revelations about an NT shadow minister’s dicey driving history — and the Opposition Leader's failure to disclose it — come as another blow to NT Labor.

image.altText: an aboriginal man wearing a suit in parliament

items.title: One of the deadliest cancers in Australia is becoming more common

children.content: Clinical worker John McNeur didn't realise he was unwell until he was delivering a health workshop on pancreatic cancer in the remote Northern Territory — and he began ticking all of the boxes. Now he's on a mission to raise awareness.

gallery.altText: A close-up portrait of a bald man looking straight at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A close-up portrait of a bald man looking straight at the camera

items.title: Man charged with murder after woman shot on the Gold Coast

children.content: A 23-year-old man is due to appear in court, charged with murder, after he allegedly shot and killed a woman on the Gold Coast.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Baby, young boys pulled alive from Venezuela earthquake rubble

children.content: The three children are among at least 33 people rescued during a weekend multinational operation that continued searching for survivors, while thousands remained unaccounted-for.

image.altText: An infant baby covered in dust and a blue blanket being carried by emergency workers in helmets from rubble.

images.altText: Stephen Eustáquio holds his head in his hands
Derek Cornelius shoots at a maze of players
Jesse Marsch celebrates in front of a Canadian huddle

children.content: World Cup co-host Canada beats South Africa to progress into the last 16 thanks to a last-gasp strike from Stephen Eustaquio in Los Angeles. Follow live.

items.title: Canada scores stunning injury time winner to down South Africa

updates.title: FT: Canada beats South Africa after stoppage-time drama
90+1 Goal for Canada!! Late drama in LA

items.title: Two things this week gave clues about the global future

children.content: Two things happened last week that give us a much clearer picture of what the world is going to be like from now on.

image.altText: Chinese President Xi Jinping attends the closing session of the National People's Congress.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Report finds 'extensive' coal and gas presence in schools and children's sport

children.content: Santos, BHP, Woodside and others have an "extensive presence" in childhood settings through more than 260 programs, a new report identifies, as senators say classroom programs and lessons written by the industry should be examined through a senate inquiry.

image.altText: Children look at a laptop. The word Glencore can be seen on the lid. A person in an orange high-vis shirt is nearby.

items.title: New laws aim to make it harder to launder ill-gotten gains through property

children.content: Anti-money laundering laws mean that for the first time, real estate agents, lawyers, accountants and others must report suspicious transactions.

articles.title: Major capital cities record worst housing auction clearance rates in years

children.content: Sydney and Melbourne record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

image.altText: An A-frame sign that reads "auction today", directing toward a house.

articles.title: Treasurer promises to 'fix' property tax oversight, but won't say how exactly

children.content: The Treasurer is promising to "fix" an unintended consequence of the government's overhaul of property tax breaks to protect widows and divorcees — but he has refused to say exactly how.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit standing in front of an Australian flag.

gallery.altText: Aerial shot of dozens of houses in neat rows in a new outer-suburban housing estate.

thumbnail.altText: Aerial shot of dozens of houses in neat rows in a new outer-suburban housing estate.

avatar.alt: ABC business reporter Daniel Ziffer in a front on profile shot wearing a grey suit and stripy tie.

items.title: Australia and Vanuatu set to sign strategic pact after months of negotiations

children.content: Vanuatu's Prime Minister Jotham Napat and Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese are set to ink the Nakamal Agreement, almost 10 months after Mr Napat pulled out of a planned signing ceremony in Port Vila, citing concerns around sovereignty.

image.altText: Two men in suits shaking hands

items.title: As Ben Stokes retires, a former captain warns 'he won't be the last'

children.content: England captain Ben Stokes will retire from international cricket after the third Test against New Zealand, the team has said.

image.altText: England captain Ben Stokes standing in the field

items.title: Do you qualify for three hours of free electricity? Here's how it works

children.content: Under the federal government's Solar Sharer scheme, some Australian households will be able to access three hours of free electricity a day. Here's how you can make the most of it.

thumbnail.altText: close up hands loading dirty clothes into washing machine at home laundry.

items.title: Thai teenager's father 'deeply saddened' as Australian charged with murder

children.content: The family of a 17-year-old girl in Thailand speak of their grief as an Australian man is charged with her murder.

image.altText: A teary man with a black tshirt slung over his shoulder, speaks into a small microphone as a woman in yellow sits beside him

items.title: History quiz: How much do you know about Eminem, Austen and elite chess?

children.content: This week was a big one in times gone by. From this decade to the 17th century, put your history knowledge to the test.

items.title: Race Around The World recap: Catch this week's films

children.content: From survival vaults in Norway to a pianist in Pisa, catch up on the films from week four of Race Around The World.

items.title: Renewed Gulf hostilities put markets on edge, ASX poised for marginal gain

children.content: Wall Street was weighed down by another bout of selling in the tech sector. The ASX is priced to edged higher despite renewed hostilities in the Gulf

image.altText: ASX

items.title: Skydiving plane crashes in France, killing all 11 people on board

children.content: A small aircraft carrying skydivers has crashed in eastern France, killing all 11 people on board, local authorities say.

gallery.altText: Rescuers work at the site of a plane crash, with the wreckage under a shade structure

thumbnail.altText: Rescuers work at the site of a plane crash, with the wreckage under a shade structure

items.title: Trump allies in the South are breaking up majority-Black electorates

children.content: States in America's South are scrambling to draw up new electoral maps that would eliminate majority-Black electorates.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit stands in front of a large building

items.title: Lawyers under pressure as court backlog hits 'crisis point'

children.content: Routine cases are dragging through Tasmanian courts for years as senior lawyers lament the "crisis" engulfing the state's criminal justice system.

image.altText: Kim Baumeler working

items.title: Ageing 'Nashos' call for priority as all veterans get equal access to health benefits

children.content: All Australian veterans will soon have access to claim compensation for health issues, fulfilling a royal commission recommendation, but those conscripted during the Vietnam War say their advanced age means their claims should receive priority.

gallery.altText: An old photo of young soldiers in a casual setting

thumbnail.altText: An old photo of young soldiers in a casual setting

items.title: Telstra phasing out CAN Radio phone system amid customer safety concerns

children.content: Telstra's Customer Access Network Radio has been a lifeline for many for more than 40 years but its technology has reached the end of its life. Customers now fear a new system will not be as reliable in an emergency.

image.altText: Telstra worker about to enter a manhole.

items.title: 'Big loss': Doctors call for fatigue policy review after car crash death

children.content: Doctors are calling for Tasmania's Department of Health to examine its fatigue management policies after a doctor died on the state's roads. Artyom Avetisyan, a cardiothoracic surgery registrar at the Royal Hobart Hospital, has been remembered by hospital staff as a kind and compassionate colleague who will be "greatly missed".

image.altText: Two buildings of the Royal Hobart Hospital with kunanyi/Mount Wellington visible in the background

items.title: The national program teaching a 'hushed topic' to boys and girls

children.content: Educators behind a national program teaching high school students about periods and pelvic pain hope it will encourage empathy.

gallery.altText: two schoolboys in blue uniforms lean against a tree smiling

thumbnail.altText: two schoolboys in blue uniforms lean against a tree smiling

items.title: Piastri has 'really good day', Russell survives late charge in Austria

children.content: Oscar Piastri has hailed his fourth place at Sunday's Austrian Grand Prix as a "really good day", while Mercedes George Russell held on to claim his first victory since the start of the season.

image.altText: Oscar Piastri holding a microphone and smiling during a media conference

items.title: Family still reeling after death of baby in regional WA

children.content: The parents of Willow Horne are still looking for answers after the eight-month-old died in a regional WA hospital.

image.altText: A baby with hands on top of balancing ball

items.title: Australian run of Waitress closing early with Sydney shows cancelled

children.content: The Australian stage production of Waitress will no longer continue on its national tour, with planned shows in Sydney now cancelled.
